current_role: Owner-operator — handles purchasing, supplier relations, and daily operations
weekly_tasks_text: Receive and manually enter supplier invoices into inventory system (3-5 invoices per week, 20-60 line items each); compare invoice prices against previous orders and flag discrepancies; update product catalog when suppliers rename or repackage items; generate weekly stock replenishment order per supplier; handle end-of-day cash reconciliation; respond to supplier queries and delivery issues; review slow-moving stock and plan promotions; train staff on POS and receiving procedures.
current_tools_text: POS system (retailer-specific, no open API), Excel spreadsheets for inventory tracking, WhatsApp for supplier communication, paper invoices from most suppliers (some email PDFs), calculator for price variance checks.
top_time_sinks_text: Manual invoice entry — typing every line item, quantity, and price from paper or PDF into the spreadsheet takes 25-40 minutes per invoice. Product name mismatches between supplier invoice and our catalog (supplier writes 'Coca-Cola 1.5L PET' we have 'Coke 1.5 bottle') mean I have to manually search and match. Price changes are easy to miss — I only catch them if I happen to remember the old price. Stock replenishment orders are built manually from memory and a quick shelf walk.
error_sensitive_areas_text: Invoice entry errors cause stock count drift — if I enter the wrong quantity we order too much or run out. Price entry errors affect margin calculations. Matching a product to the wrong SKU corrupts the inventory history. Any committed inventory update is hard to reverse once staff have processed it.
customer_facing_areas_text: Pricing at checkout (POS). Promotions and shelf labels. Out-of-stock situations — customers notice immediately. Staff interactions at the register.
primary_goal_text: Reduce the time spent processing supplier invoices from roughly 2-3 hours per week to under 1 hour, and stop missing supplier price changes that quietly erode my margins.
things_owner_refuses_to_automate_text: Any inventory update committed to the POS without my explicit approval. Final purchase order sent to a supplier. Pricing changes on the shelf — I decide those.
measurement_context_text: Task: supplier invoice processing. I receive about 4 invoices per week across my main suppliers. Each invoice has roughly 30 line items on average. A typical invoice takes me about 35 minutes end to end — entering line items, checking prices, matching products. I'd say about 70% of that time is mechanical (typing, searching for the right product name) and 30% is judgment (spotting price changes, deciding if a product name mismatch is the same item or a new one). I catch a pricing mistake or product mismatch roughly 1 in every 3 invoices. Fixing one takes about 10 minutes once I realise it. If AI handled the mechanical entry and matching, I think I'd still want to review every invoice before it updates stock — so 100% review for now, until I trust it. 'This worked' would mean: I open the invoice, AI gives me a pre-filled draft, I scan it in under 5 minutes and approve. I'd stop using it if the draft has more than 2 wrong product matches per invoice and I'm spending more time correcting than I would have entering manually.
